game_name: Street Fighter II Turbo: Hyper Fighting

danger_signals.visual_form: character pose freeze and visual charging effect
danger_signals.meaning: high-damage attack incoming, requires block or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: red health bar segment decreasing in size
danger_signals.meaning: cumulative damage taken, approaching knockout state

danger_signals.visual_form: character sprite on ground in prone position
danger_signals.meaning: temporary vulnerability, opponent can press advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: character guard pose with raised arms/weapon
character positioned at mid-screen range away from opponent
safety_signals.meaning: breathing room, can prepare counterattack or recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: character in neutral ready position between actions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: character select screen flags representing country of origin
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: alternate costume palettes per character for versus differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: stage backgrounds representing fighter's homeland (e.g., Japan for Ryu, Soviet Union for Zangief, India for Dhalsim, China for Chun-Li)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Grand Master designation (M. Bison, Sagat, Vega, Balrog)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: distinct sprite designs and move sets differentiate bosses from regular roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: final stage backgrounds darker and more imposing than earlier stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: full health bar display, bright character palette, upright stance animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: full mobility, access to all special moves, faster attack recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: partially depleted health bar with red segment missing, character remains in default palette but animations show wear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: same move set available but psychological pressure increases as knockout approaches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: health bar critically low (final segment), no visual sprite change but status is unambiguous
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: one combo sequence away from loss, requires defensive play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: stage background static but distinct per fighter encountered; final bosses fought on darker stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI difficulty ramps with each successive opponent; boss characters have unique move sets unavailable to player roster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both character palettes available for customization; alternate palettes accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: special moves can be toggled on/off via cheat codes; speed settings adjustable (up to six levels)

environmental_storytelling.example: Japan dojo stage for Ryu; Soviet Union industrial backdrop for Zangief; India temple for Dhalsim; China streets for Chun-Li. Each environment reinforces fighter's cultural/national origin.
Grand Master encounters occur on darker, more ominous stage backgrounds compared to regular fighter stages, signaling narrative escalation and increased threat level.
Each fighter receives new default palette in Turbo, with original palettes relegated to alternate slots. This visual refresh signals narrative/timeline progression from Champion Edition.
Special moves introduced/expanded for each character (e.g., Chun-Li gains Kikoken projectile, Dhalsim gains Yoga Teleport). New abilities visually differentiate Turbo iteration from predecessor.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: idle stance with bouncing/breathing animation; responds immediately to input
specific pose freeze followed by attack sprite; higher input precision required in Turbo due to increased game speed
prone sprite on ground; temporary invulnerability during ground animation before stand-up recovery
forward movement, frequent special move attempts, minimal blocking
blocking stance held longer, spacing control via movement, reactive rather than proactive
unique move set with attacks unavailable to playable roster; specific combo patterns; higher damage output and priority
voice clips call out country names and round progression; sound effects added in SNES version (people/animals shouting) signal emotional/narrative stakes

notes: Street Fighter II Turbo is a pure competitive fighting game with minimal narrative world-state. Manifestation patterns are entirely match-mechanical rather than environmental. Danger/safety signals derive from health status, positioning, and animation frames. Faction identity is primarily national/cultural through stage backgrounds and character origin flags. The game communicates state through sprite animation frames, health bar visuals, and sound design (pitch variation in voice clips for special move strength levels, though removed in SNES port). The Turbo iteration's increased game speed fundamentally changes visual read patterns—animations compress, requiring faster visual parsing. Boss encounters use stage background darkness as a tonal signal of increased threat. Alternate palettes serve as cosmetic faction markers in versus mode.
